content.text:
<user_query>
explain why I get this error when running e2e tests. It _should_ be mocking all api endpoints:
[WebServer] [server] OrgApiError: Internal Server Error
[WebServer]     at requestOrgApiJson (/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts:84:24)
[WebServer]     at Object.serverFn (/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/server/app-bootstrap.ts?tss-serverfn-split:9:12)
[WebServer]     at server (/Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/@tanstack/start-client-core/src/createServerFn.ts:797:24)
[WebServer]     at callNextMiddleware (/Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/@tanstack/start-client-core/src/createServerFn.ts:288:24)
[WebServer]     at Object.assign.__executeServer (/Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/@tanstack/start-client-core/src/createServerFn.ts:170:28)
[WebServer]     at client (/Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/@tanstack/start-client-core/src/createServerFn.ts:791:21)
[WebServer]     at callNextMiddleware (/Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/@tanstack/start-client-core/src/createServerFn.ts:288:24)
[WebServer]     at Object.assign (/Users/duhl/git/ui/node_modules/@tanstack/start-client-core/src/createServerFn.ts:125:26)
[WebServer]     at fetchAppBootstrapData (/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/server/app-bootstrap.ts?tss-serverfn-split:20:16)
[WebServer]     at Object.serverFn (/Users/duhl/git/ui/apps/org-next/src/server/app-bootstrap.ts?tss-serverfn-split:44:26) {
[WebServer]   status: 500
[WebServer] } {
[WebServer]   method: 'GET',
[WebServer]   orgApiErrorResponse: true,
[WebServer]   path: '/users/me',
[WebServer]   responseBody: 'Internal Server Error',
[WebServer]   status: 500,
[WebServer]   url: 'http://localhost:3000/users/me'
[WebServer] }
</user_query>
I'm diving into the codebase to get a sense of how everything's structured.

This log is expected test noise — not a broken mock. It comes from [apps/org-next/tests/root-error.spec.ts](apps/org-next/tests/root-error.spec.ts), which deliberately mocks `/users/me` as a 500 to exercise the root error boundary.

## What's happening

1. The test intentionally returns HTTP 500 from `/users/me` via `request-mocking-protocol` (RMP).
2. That mock is honored correctly. In [apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts), `requestOrgApiJson` sees `!response.ok` with status 500, constructs an `OrgApiError`, and — because the status is not 401 — calls `logServerErrorToRollbar` before rethrowing:

```80:98:apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts
    if (!response.ok) {
      const errorText = await response.text();
      const status = response.status;
      const message = errorText || `Request failed for ${path}`;
      const apiError = new OrgApiError(message, status);

      if (!shouldSkipRollbarForStatus(status)) {
        logServerErrorToRollbar(apiError, {
          method,
          orgApiErrorResponse: true,
          path,
          responseBody: truncateForLog(errorText || ""),
          status,
          url: url.toString(),
        });
      }

      throw apiError;
    }
```

3. In dev/test, `logServerErrorToRollbar` logs to stderr, which Playwright prints prefixed with `[WebServer]`. The `OrgApiError` itself then propagates up through `fetchCurrentUser` → `fetchAppBootstrapData` → `getAppBootstrapData` (matching the stack trace you pasted), where the `_authed` loader renders `RootError`. That's exactly what `root-error.spec.ts` asserts.

4. The docs explicitly call this out as expected:

```133:136:apps/org-next/docs/README.md
- `root-error.spec.ts` intentionally mocks `/users/me` as 500, which causes
  the server to log `OrgApiError: Internal Server Error` via
  `logServerErrorToRollbar` during the test run. This log line is expected
  test noise, not a broken mock.
```

## How to confirm it's not a real unmocked call

A few quick checks if you want to be sure:

- The error's `url` is `http://localhost:3000/users/me`. That's the dev web server origin, not `API_HOSTNAME`. RMP intercepts the outbound `fetch` and serves the mocked response — the logged URL is whatever was passed to `fetch`, which is `new URL("/users/me", env.API_HOSTNAME)`. In test env `API_HOSTNAME` is set to `http://localhost:3000` (see [apps/org-next/playwright.config.ts](apps/org-next/playwright.config.ts) / `.env.test` if present), so the URL shape alone doesn't mean the mock missed.
- The `responseBody` is exactly `'Internal Server Error'` — that's the literal body the test sets, not a real upstream 500. An unmocked request would either fail to connect or return a different body.
- It only appears while `root-error.spec.ts` runs. If you see it during other specs, then something is off; otherwise it's working as designed.

## If you want to silence it

Options, from lightest to heaviest:
- Suppress in the test itself by scoping Rollbar logging off for that spec (e.g. have `logServerErrorToRollbar` no-op when `VITE_ENABLE_RMP` is true and a header flag says "this 500 is expected").
- Add a "silent" variant to the mock (e.g. include a custom header the server inspects in `requestOrgApiJson` and skips `logServerErrorToRollbar` for).
- Filter `[WebServer]` stderr in Playwright's `webServer.stdout` / test runner reporter.

Do you want me to plan one of those silencing options, or is confirming this is expected noise enough? If silencing, which approach do you prefer:

 I found the documentation explaining this—root-error.spec.ts intentionally mocks the /users/me endpoint to return a 500 error, which triggers the expected server error logging during the test. This is documented as known test noise, not an actual issue with the mocks.

questions.prompt: You said the tests 'should be mocking all api endpoints' — what outcome do you want?

options.label: Just confirming this is expected noise from root-error.spec.ts — no changes needed
Silence this specific expected log in root-error.spec.ts only
Suppress Rollbar-style server error logs globally when running under Playwright/RMP

content.text:
<user_query>
I still don't understand how this is getting to the console output. Do we console.log somewhere or what?
</user_query>
I need to check the rollbar server file to see what's causing this console output.

<user_query>
I still don't understand how this is getting to the console output. Do we console.log somewhere or what?
</user_query>

Yes — there's a literal `console.error` call. It's the only reason that line reaches stdout/stderr.

Here's the exact source, one file, one line:

```22:22:apps/org-next/src/server/rollbar.server.ts
    console.error("[server]", error, customData);
```

That `console.error` is what produces the log. Nothing else in the stack writes to the console for this path.

## Why that line runs during tests

`logServerErrorToRollbar` has two branches:

```15:24:apps/org-next/src/server/rollbar.server.ts
export const logServerErrorToRollbar = (
  error: Error | string,
  customData?: Record<string, unknown>,
) => {
  if (isServerRollbarEnabled) {
    rollbar.error(error, customData);
  } else {
    console.error("[server]", error, customData);
  }
};
```

And `isServerRollbarEnabled` requires production + a real token:

```4:5:apps/org-next/src/server/rollbar.server.ts
export const isServerRollbarEnabled =
  process.env.NODE_ENV === "production" && !!env.VITE_ROLLBAR_ACCESS_TOKEN;
```

Under Playwright, `NODE_ENV` is not `"production"`, so `isServerRollbarEnabled` is `false` → the `else` branch runs → `console.error` fires.

## How the text you're seeing gets assembled

Reading your log line by line:

- `[WebServer]` — added by Playwright. Its `webServer` config pipes the dev server's stdout/stderr into the test runner and prefixes each line with `[WebServer]`. Not our code.
- `[server]` — the first argument to `console.error` above.
- `OrgApiError: Internal Server Error` — Node's default formatting of the `Error` object (second argument). It prints `name: message`, then the stack on following lines.
- Stack trace lines (`at requestOrgApiJson …`, `at fetchAppBootstrapData …`, etc.) — Node's default `Error.stack` output, still part of the same `console.error` call.
- The trailing `{ method, orgApiErrorResponse, path, responseBody, status, url }` object — the third argument (`customData`) to `console.error`, formatted by `util.inspect`.
- The `{ status: 500 }` object in the middle — that's Node printing the own enumerable properties of the `OrgApiError` instance (it has a `status` field assigned in the constructor), also part of the `Error` argument.

So the full `[WebServer] [server] OrgApiError: … { status: 500 } { method: 'GET', … }` is a single `console.error(...)` call's output, with Playwright's `[WebServer]` prefix on every wrapped line.

## Who triggers it

[apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts) lines 86–95 call `logServerErrorToRollbar` whenever a non-401 non-OK response comes back from the Org API. The Playwright test [apps/org-next/tests/root-error.spec.ts](apps/org-next/tests/root-error.spec.ts) mocks `/users/me` to return 500 on purpose (to verify the root error boundary), which is a non-401 non-OK response, which hits that branch, which calls `console.error`.

So the chain end-to-end is:

```
root-error.spec.ts mocks /users/me = 500
  -> requestOrgApiJson sees !response.ok, status 500
  -> logServerErrorToRollbar(apiError, {...})
  -> isServerRollbarEnabled === false (not prod)
  -> console.error("[server]", error, customData)    <-- the only console write
  -> Node writes to stderr of the dev server process
  -> Playwright's webServer pipes stderr into the test output with "[WebServer]" prefix
  -> You see it.
```

That's the full path. No other `console.log`/`console.error` is involved for this line.

 The user is asking the same question again, so I need to be clearer about where the error appears. There's a `console.error` call in `rollbar.server.ts` at line 22 that's responsible for the console output they're seeing.

This log is expected test noise — not a broken mock. It comes from [apps/org-next/tests/root-error.spec.ts](apps/org-next/tests/root-error.spec.ts), which deliberately mocks `/users/me` as a 500 to exercise the root error boundary.

2. That mock is honored correctly. In [apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts](apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts), `requestOrgApiJson` sees `!response.ok` with status 500, constructs an `OrgApiError`, and — because the status is not 401 — calls `logServerErrorToRollbar` before rethrowing:

```80:98:apps/org-next/src/server/org-api.server.ts
      const errorText = await response.text();

      if (!shouldSkipRollbarForStatus(status)) {
        logServerErrorToRollbar(apiError, {

          path,
          responseBody: truncateForLog(errorText || ""),

```

The `OrgApiError` itself then propagates up through `fetchCurrentUser` → `fetchAppBootstrapData` → `getAppBootstrapData` (matching the stack trace you pasted), where the `_authed` loader renders `RootError`. That's exactly what `root-error.spec.ts` asserts.

- Filter `[WebServer]` stderr in Playwright's `webServer.stdout` / test runner reporter.
